name: Hati and Skoll
description: Hati Hróðvitnisson and Sköll are two wolves in Norse mythology. Hati chases Máni, the moon, across the night sky, while Sköll pursues Sól, the sun, during the day. According to prophecy, they will eventually catch their prey, triggering the events of Ragnarök. Hati is often understood to be the son of Fenrir, and Sköll is his brother. Their constant pursuit of the celestial bodies is a driving force in the Norse cosmos, representing the relentless march of time towards the end of the world. They are symbols of chaos and the ever-present threat to the established order. The wolves' hunger is insatiable, and their relentless chase is a constant reminder of the inevitable doom foretold in the prophecies. Their capture of the sun and moon will plunge the world into darkness, signaling the beginning of the final battle between gods and giants.
subtitle: The wolves who chase the sun and moon
appearance: Hati and Sköll are depicted as gigantic wolves, their fur often described as dark and wild. Their eyes gleam with an insatiable hunger, reflecting the light of the moon and sun they pursue. They are powerful and imposing figures, radiating an aura of primal ferocity. Their size is such that they can be seen across the sky as they chase their prey, a constant reminder of the coming end.
habitat: The celestial realm, constantly pursuing the sun and moon across the sky.
behavior: Hati and Sköll are driven by an insatiable hunger to devour the sun and moon. They are relentless in their pursuit, never resting and always striving to catch their prey. They embody chaos and the inevitable approach of Ragnarök. Their behavior is purely instinctual, driven by the prophecy that dictates their existence.
weaknesses: Prophecy (They are bound to fulfill their role in Ragnarök)
Divine Intervention (Potentially vulnerable to powerful gods)

keyMyths.title: The Chase of Sól and Máni
keyMyths.description: Hati chases the moon Máni and Sköll chases the sun Sól across the sky. They will eventually catch them, plunging the world into darkness before Ragnarök.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, providing details about Hati and Sköll's role in the cosmos.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, providing details about Hati and Sköll's role in the cosmos.

sources.author: Various
